raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":1.0,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis and contribution framing, especially around unsupervised anomaly detection and the principal deliverable claim.","R1.2":"The artifact contains potentially conflicting argumentative commitments, so checking whether the setup, method, experiments, and deliverable claims cohere is load-bearing.","R1.3":"The request centrally requires judging whether reported experiments support claims such as full unsupervision, formal guarantees, benchmark contribution, and empirical gains.","R1.4":"Because the artifact makes first-work and no-prior-work claims, assessing novelty relative to prior work is a central expected responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projection of broad feedback must disclose uncertainty about what can be inferred from the limited excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The response must avoid amplifying first, guaranteed, or fully unsupervised claims beyond what the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibility and not provide the actual critique requested by the delegation.","RX.4":"The projection should account for risks from misleading scientific claims or unsupported benchmark/method assertions.","RX.5":"The projection must keep responsibility grounded in specific claims visible in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of the thesis and contribution framing, especially around unsupervised anomaly detection and the principal deliverable claim.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains potentially conflicting argumentative commitments, so checking whether the setup, method, experiments, and deliverable claims cohere is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The request centrally requires judging whether reported experiments support claims such as full unsupervision, formal guarantees, benchmark contribution, and empirical gains.
rationale.R1.4: Because the artifact makes first-work and no-prior-work claims, assessing novelty relative to prior work is a central expected responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of broad feedback must disclose uncertainty about what can be inferred from the limited excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The response must avoid amplifying first, guaranteed, or fully unsupervised claims beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibility and not provide the actual critique requested by the delegation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for risks from misleading scientific claims or unsupported benchmark/method assertions.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must keep responsibility grounded in specific claims visible in the provided artifact.
